question: Can the split selected-slot dQ/no-dKV launch and union/group-aware dKV-only launch overlap on two streams, and do we need an end sync?
read: The two-stream A/B is real and correctness-equivalent, but it regresses latency because both launches replay the same QK/P/scaled-dP source-boundary work and compete for the same CUs, MFMA issue, L2/HBM, allocator zero-fill kernels, and dKV write path.

The serialized split is now a narrow latency win over the retained 11.13 ms gate, but it still recomputes QK/P/scaled-dP twice and is a two-launch diagnostic path, not the final fused/cooperative sparse-attention backward owner.

The benchmark waits on both side streams before recording the end event so the measured value represents completed device work. In a production autograd path this should be represented as event dependencies or stream recording for the downstream consumers, not a host-wide synchronization.
next: If using split as a temporary integration candidate, measure materialized TorchTitan sparse-attention boundary and then e2e TFLOPs; do not assume the 10.74 ms standalone number survives bridge overhead.
For the real kernel, keep the selected-slot query-major dQ path and move group-aware dKV reduction into the same producer/cooperative boundary so QK/P/scaled-dP is computed once.
CU-masked separation would require a HIP/CK-level owner or explicit hipExtStreamCreateWithCUMask-style stream construction; PyTorch/Triton stream launches do not expose a clean CU/LDS partition knob.
